title: JAK inhibitor — implied-by-data and partial support
notes: Synthetic; reference 1 is a data table with no narrative sentence (implied_by_data). VERIFY before publishing. (Avoids named endpoints like 'ACR20' that the citation normalizer mis-reads as a citation marker — see findings note.)
manuscript: At week 24, the JAK inhibitor produced a higher clinical response rate than placebo.1 The JAK inhibitor improved physical function and reduced fatigue.2

goldClaims.originalText: At week 24, the JAK inhibitor produced a higher clinical response rate than placebo.1
goldClaims.text: At week 24, the JAK inhibitor produced a higher clinical response rate than placebo

goldClaims.originalText: The JAK inhibitor improved physical function and reduced fatigue.2
goldClaims.text: The JAK inhibitor improved physical function and reduced fatigue

1.name: RA-JAK table (Singh 2023)
1.text: In this 24-week trial, 350 patients with rheumatoid arthritis were randomised to the JAK inhibitor or placebo. Response outcomes are summarised in Table 3. At week 24, a clinical response was recorded in 62% of patients in the JAK-inhibitor group and 25% in the placebo group. Background therapy was unchanged during the study.

2.name: RA-JAK secondary endpoints
2.text: Patient-reported outcomes were pre-specified secondary endpoints. Physical function, measured by HAQ-DI, improved significantly with the JAK inhibitor versus placebo (p<0.001). Fatigue scores (FACIT-Fatigue) were numerically better with the JAK inhibitor, but the difference did not reach statistical significance.

goldVerdicts.claimText: At week 24, the JAK inhibitor produced a higher clinical response rate than placebo
goldVerdicts.rationale: Support is a data table (62% vs 25%) with no narrative sentence stating the comparison (rule 5).

goldVerdicts.claimText: The JAK inhibitor improved physical function and reduced fatigue
goldVerdicts.rationale: Physical function improvement is explicit and significant; fatigue is non-significant — compound claim, one half holds (rule 4).
